member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.CombatFrameEventArgs
member.summary: Fired every frame in FN_TURN_CONTROL_UPDATE. Must match Core's CombatFrameEventArgs layout. Set SpeedMultiplier and SpeedDirty=1 to override combat animation speed.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.DamageEventArgs

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.StatRecomputeEventArgs

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.ItemBreakEventArgs

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.ClassLockedEventArgs

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.BrainFactoryEventArgs

member.summary: Fired when a tactical fight ends (FN_FIGHT_END, RVA 0x3BA820), real fights only. Result: 0 = Win (player won), 1 = Lose (player lost).

member.summary: Fired when FN_FIGHT_TRANSITION is called with "embark" (cats leave on adventure).

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.AdventureReturnEventArgs
member.summary: Fired when FN_FIGHT_TRANSITION is called with "return" or "return_early". IsEarly=1 means the adventure was cut short.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.HouseBossIntroEventArgs
member.summary: Fired when FN_FIGHT_TRANSITION is called with "house_boss_intro".

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Api.RunCoinChangedEventArgs
member.summary: Fired each combat frame when the adventure coin count changes (polled from inv+328). OldCoins and NewCoins are the coin counts before and after the change. Delta = NewCoins - OldCoins (positive = gained, negative = spent).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Fight.GetFighters
member.summary:
Returns all real combatants in the current fight (player cats and enemies). Static terrain objects and inanimate collectibles are excluded. Use to get the raw unfiltered list.
Pointer chain from TurnControl: ctx = *(tc + 24) entity = *(ctx + 8) state_blk = *(entity + 32) char_list = *(state_blk + 8048) — ref-counted container count = *(int*)(char_list + 12) data = *(ptr*)(char_list + 16) — Character*[count] at 8-byte stride

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Fight.GetAllEntities
member.summary: Returns every entity in the TurnControl list, including static terrain and inanimate objects. Prefer for actual combatants.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap
member.summary: Known RVAs and struct offsets for Mewgenics. ⚠ KEEP IN SYNC WITH MewgenicsCore\GameAddressMap.cs — update both files when RVAs or offsets change. Game executable MD5: 4719cff5087d4cba9aaf54db878309ce

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_TURN_START
member.summary: sub_1408DE9F0 — TurnStart: fired once per character turn (4474 bytes). RCX = TurnControl ptr.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GET_ACTIVE_FIGHTER
member.summary: sub_140048F40 — get_active_fighter(TurnControl* tc, _, _) → CombatFighter*. Returns the CombatFighter for the character currently taking their turn. Called by TurnStart at line 693. Pass TurnControl ptr as a1; a2/a3 can be 0. Confirmed CombatFighter offsets: Level +0x120, HP +0x16C, MaxHP +0x17C, Stats +0x190 stride-8.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_ADD_FURNITURE
member.summary: sub_140202560 — AddFurniture(HouseInventory* inv, glaiel::string* type_name, bool check_rare). Allocates a glaiel::FurniturePieceEntry (104 bytes), assigns a unique type_id from the global furniture counter (RVA_FURNITURE_COUNTER), copies type_name, optionally reads "can_be_rare" from GON and sets the rare flag, then appends the entry to the HouseInventory furniture array (capacity +56, count +60, ptr +64). Returns the new type_id (int64). Position (pos_x/pos_y) and rotation are 0 — the item is in inventory, not placed.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GPAK_READ_GON
member.summary: sub_1400x5FDA0 — gpak_read_gon(gpak_object* gpak, glaiel_string* path, bool unk, bool append_mode) → gon_node*. Reads a GON file from the in-process GPAK by path and returns its root node. The GPAK object to pass is at . Note: the GON tree is already parsed in memory at startup; this is a lookup/iterator, not a disk read on every call.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GON_DEFAULT_CTOR
member.summary: GonObject::default_ctor — zero-inits a GonObject, sets SSO caps and ctrl=singleton.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GON_INSERT_CHILD
member.summary: GonObject::insert_child(parent, key_str*, child_gon*) — inserts child into parent's hash+vector.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GON_HASH_INSERT
member.summary: sub_140120850 — Swiss-table hash insert for GonObject. Signature: (parent_gon*, result_buf[3], key_glaiel_string*). Inserts key into parent's hash table and returns slot info in result_buf. result_buf[1] = pointer to the new hash entry; write int child_index at result_buf[1]+32. Called by insert_child after the deep copy to update the PARENT's table. We use it to rebuild hash tables of deep-copied children (which are left empty by sub_1400285E0).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GON_HASH_LOOKUP
member.summary: sub_140936540 — GonObject hash lookup (operator[]). Signature: (parent_gon*, key_glaiel_string*) → GonObject*. Returns child GonObject* if found, or a sentinel GonObject* with type=0 if not. Used in sub_140886B20 to look up passives in the SpawnDB passives catalog.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GAME_MALLOC
member.summary: game's operator new / malloc equivalent.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_ITEM_DB_LOAD
member.summary: SpawnDatabase item loader (RVA 0x8082B0) — loads all item/spawn data from GPAK into the database. Takes a SpawnDatabase* as first argument (rcx). Has 6 callers; called at startup and on save load. WARNING: calling this mid-game requires a valid SpawnDatabase* pointer. Do not call without fully identifying the argument layout. ⚠ RVA not confirmed for current exe — do not use until verified.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.RVA_HOUSE_GLOBAL
member.summary: RVA of the HouseInventory global pointer.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.RVA_WORLD_PTR
member.summary: RVA of the glaiel::MewDirector global pointer. The MewDirector is the main game manager. It holds current_day (offset +1408) which is the day counter since the start of the run.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.RVA_GPAK_GLOBAL
member.summary:
RVA of the glaiel::gpak object itself (static global, NOT a pointer to it). The GPAK object is located directly at gameBase + RVA_GPAK_GLOBAL. Callers use: lea rcx,[gameBase + RVA_GPAK_GLOBAL] (no dereference).
RE: from live disassembly of two FN_GPAK_READ_GON callers (CE verified): (see updated export.json) → → 0x13B3240 7FF764BC9988: lea rcx,[7FF765EF7880] (same, second caller) GPAK count=457, sentinel=0x16BBFE5F6E0, 512 buckets — all verified live.
All GON data (items, abilities, etc.) is pre-parsed into this object at startup. The .gon.patch / .gon.append / .gon.merge files in the GPAK archive are also applied at startup — they are NOT file-system mod hooks.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.RVA_SPAWN_DB_INSTANCE
member.summary: Global pointer to the SpawnDatabase heap instance. *(gameBase + RVA_SPAWN_DB_INSTANCE) → SpawnDatabase*

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_SPAWN_DB_INIT
member.summary: SpawnDatabase catalog loader — sub_1407A4910. Signature: void __fastcall(SpawnDatabase* this) Loads ALL GON catalogs from the GPAK cache into SpawnDB's embedded GonObjects. ⚠ DO NOT call at runtime — secondary structures accumulate → crash ("Class X defined in multiple places"). Use NativeGonWriter.SetNumberAt / SetStringAt on SD+SPAWN_DB_ITEMS_OFFSET instead. ⚠ 0x7A7E10 was WRONG (UI settings refresh) — correct RVA is 0x7A4910.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_SPAWN_DB_GET
member.summary: sub_140319900 — get-or-create the per-character SpawnDatabase component. Signature: SpawnDatabase* __fastcall(TacticsTest* a1, nint a2, nint a3) Called by sub_14082FB70 (combat init) once per character to obtain the SpawnDB that attach_passive reads from. Has a fast-path: if the entity already has a SpawnDB component cached, it returns it without calling FN_SPAWN_DB_INIT. Hooking the RETURN of this function is the only reliable injection point for both fresh and cached SpawnDB instances.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_ATTACH_PASSIVE
member.summary:
sub_140491B90 — glaiel::attach_passive (ConstructionHelpers.cpp, line 175). Signature: glaiel::Passive* __fastcall(glaiel_string* name, glaiel::Character* charPtr, const GonObject* gon, bool flag) This is the REAL dispatch table: maps built-in effect-type names ("StrengthUp", "Thorns", "StatusOnKill", etc.) to C++ Passive constructors. Logs "NO PASSIVE NAMED X" (uppercase) when X is not a known built-in type.
When an item has passives { ModBloodrage 1 }, the game calls: attach_passive("ModBloodrage", char, gon{1}, false) "ModBloodrage" is not a built-in type → "NO PASSIVE NAMED ModBloodrage".
Hook this to intercept custom named passive definitions: 1. Check SpawnDB passives catalog for the name. 2. If found: read the level from gon (int_data at +0x50), look up level sub-object, iterate over its "passives" children, call original for each sub-passive. 3. If not found: call original (handles all built-in types).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_LANG_OUTER_NODE
member.summary: sub_1409584C0 — find-or-create outer language-map node for a given language code. Signature: nint fn(nint langMapBase, nint scratch16, nint langGlaielStr) langMapBase = gameBase + RVA_TEXT_MANAGER (outer language map) scratch16 = pointer to 16-byte writable buffer langGlaielStr = glaiel_string* holding language code ("en", "fr", "de", etc.) Returns scratch16; scratch16[0] = outer_node ptr (the lang node). outer_node + 0x30 = inner hash map (text_key → glaiel_wstring). RE: called by sub_14095DCD0 (core getText) to navigate lang → inner_map.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_LANG_INNER_NODE
member.summary: sub_140958010 — find-or-create inner text-key node within a language's inner map. Signature: nint fn(nint innerMapBase, nint scratch16, nint keyGlaielStr) innerMapBase = outer_node + 0x30 (inner hash map for a specific language) scratch16 = pointer to 16-byte writable buffer keyGlaielStr = glaiel_string* holding text key (e.g. "MOD_BLOODBLADE_NAME") Returns scratch16; scratch16[0] = inner_node ptr. inner_node + 0x30 = glaiel_wstring (UTF-16 display text). RE: display read path uses sub_14004BDA0; write path uses this function (create variant).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_WSTRING_ASSIGN
member.summary: sub_14005AA50 — glaiel_wstring assign from (wchar_t* src, size_t len). Signature: void fn(nint wstrBase, nint srcWchar, nuint wcharCount) wstrBase = pointer to glaiel_wstring (32B: SSO buf[16], len, cap) srcWchar = pointer to wchar_t array (UTF-16, NOT null-terminated) wcharCount = number of wchar_t chars (NOT bytes) SSO inline for ≤7 wchar_t; heap-allocates for longer strings.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.OFF_LANG_CURRENT
member.summary: Current language glaiel_string — at gameBase + OFF_LANG_CURRENT (= RVA_TEXT_MANAGER + 0x40). Holds the active language code as UTF-8 ("en", "fr", "de", "it", "sp", "pt-br"). Always SSO (language codes ≤ 5 bytes). Read-only at runtime.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.RVA_TEXT_MANAGER
member.summary:
Outer language map — located directly at gameBase + RVA_TEXT_MANAGER (NOT a pointer). RE: dword_1413B8C90 → RVA 0x13B8C90.
This is the top-level display-text data structure. Layout: outer hash map keyed by language code ("en", "fr", etc.) → outer_node (0x70 bytes). outer_node + 0x30 = inner hash map (text_key → inner_node, 0x50 bytes). inner_node + 0x30 = glaiel_wstring (UTF-16 display text).
Text display pipeline: 1. CSV files → ICU processing → CSVResource (cached in generic map at OFF_TEXT_CATALOG) 2. sub_14095D6A0 populates THIS map from CSVResource ICU buffers 3. sub_140986B70 (draw) calls sub_14095DCD0 (getText) which reads from THIS map
For mod injection, bypass steps 1+2 and write directly into this map via + + .
Current language glaiel_string is at (this+0x40).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.MEWDIRECTOR_PARTY_CAPACITY
member.summary: Adventure party array — three consecutive fields at MewDirector+1464. RE source: sub_1403B8B60 (init adventure on save load) reads/writes these fields. MEWDIRECTOR_PARTY_COUNT is the number of cat IDs currently in the party (0 when no adventure is active; populated after save-load when on_adventure is set). MEWDIRECTOR_PARTY_DATA is a raw pointer to a heap array of int64 cat IDs, one per slot. int32 capacity at MEWDIRECTOR_PARTY_CAPACITY is managed internally; do not write.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_BREED_CATS
member.summary: sub_1400D6310 — breed_cats(CatDB* catdb, long momId, long dadId, void* familyTree, int flag) → char_ptr. Creates a new kitten from two parent CatIds. Calls FN_GENETICS_COMPUTE internally. familyTree can safely be null. Returns the new kitten's char_ptr (0 on failure). ⚠️ Call only from OnHouseUpdate — same threading requirement as DeleteCat.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_CATDB_FIND
member.summary: sub_1400D5480 — catdb_find(CatDB* catdb, long cat_id) → char_ptr.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_IS_ON_ADVENTURE
member.summary: sub_1403B8B00 — bool is_on_adventure(MewDirector* a1). Returns true when the adventure party has departed (on_adventure property != 0), false if cats are still in the adventure box (queued but not yet departed), or if the current scene is a CutScene, or if ChapterState (a1+1756) != 0. Confirmed via sub_1403B8B00 pseudocode: reads on_adventure from property map at a1+56.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_CREATE_ENTITY
member.summary: sub_14095AFE0 — glaiel_entity_alloc(house_obj) → entity_ptr (64 bytes). Allocates a glaiel::Entity, sets its vtable, and registers it with house_obj+8. Must be paired with FN_SPAWN_CAT_ENTITY to attach it to a cat.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_SPAWN_CAT_ENTITY
member.summary: spawn_cat_entity(house_obj, entity_ptr, cat_id_ptr) → entity_ptr. Creates the ECS/visual entity for a cat in the house scene. cat_id_ptr is a pointer to the long CatId (e.g. char_ptr + CHAR_CAT_ID). Returns 0 if the house is not ready (house_obj+1200 guard). Must be called on the game thread after FN_BREED_CATS to make the kitten visible. ⚠ RVA not confirmed for current exe — do not use until verified.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_DELETE_CAT

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_REMOVE_CAT_FROM_GAME
member.summary: sub_1401FCF20 — remove_cat_from_game(NpcMapEntity* entity). Full cleanup including ECS entity destruction.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_ADD_TO_CONTAINER
member.summary: sub_1402E6120 — container_add(NpcMapContainer* container, NpcMapEntity* entity). Adds entity to container's internal entity list. Automatically removes entity from its current container first if it is already in one. Writes *(entity + 232) = container (back-ptr). ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.SDL_ADD_EVENT_WATCH
member.summary: SDL_AddEventWatch(SDL_EventFilter filter, void* userdata) → bool. Registers a filter callback that is called for every event before the game processes it. The callback signature (Cdecl) is: byte callback(void* userdata, SDL_Event* event). Returning 1 keeps all events in the queue; returning 0 removes the event. Called from the thread that drives SDL_PollEvent (game render/main thread).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.SDL_GET_KEYBOARD_STATE
member.summary: SDL_GetKeyboardState(int* numkeys) → const byte*. Returns a pointer to a static bool array indexed by SDL_Scancode (up to ~512 entries). Array is valid for the lifetime of the process; non-zero = key held down.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.SDL_GET_MOUSE_STATE
member.summary: SDL_GetMouseState(float* x, float* y) → uint. Writes the current mouse position (window-relative) to x/y and returns a bitmask of pressed buttons: bit 0=Left, bit 1=Middle, bit 2=Right (SDL_BUTTON_MASK(n) = 1 << (n-1)).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_GET_ENTITY_LIST
member.summary: sub_1400AD730 — get_entity_list(housePtr, nint* listRef, 0). Queries ECS for NpcMapEntity list (typeId=1091).

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_ENTITY_LIST_RELEASE
member.summary: sub_140047210 — entity_list_release(nint* listRef). Must be called after get_entity_list.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_FIND_CONTAINER_BY_POS
member.summary: sub_1401E38E0 — find_container_by_pos(housePtr, double* xy, 0) → NpcMapContainer*. Returns the room container whose bounding box contains the given (X, Y) position. Container bounding box queried via vtable[28] on each container. Returns 0 if no container owns the position (e.g. outdoor / exterior area). ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_FIND_CONTAINER_BY_NAME
member.summary: sub_1401E4040 — find_container_by_name(housePtr, glaiel::string* roomName) → NpcMapContainer*. Returns the room container with the given room name string, or 0 if not found. Called by the game at save-load to restore cat positions from the house_state stream. Room names: "Floor1_Large", "Floor1_Small", "Attic", "Basement0"–"Basement4". ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_HOUSE_ADD_UPGRADE
member.summary: sub_140204A20 — HouseInventory::AddUpgrade(HouseInventory* inv, glaiel::string* key). Appends one upgrade key to the in-memory upgrade vector (with built-in deduplication) and immediately spawns the corresponding NpcMapContainers via sub_140204860. Calling multiple times is safe — containers are fully rebuilt each call. RE: HouseInventory::Load (0x203A40) calls this per upgrade key after reading the blob. ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_STRING_COPY
member.summary: sub_140051E40 — glaiel::string copy-assign(glaiel::string* dest, glaiel::string* src). Deep-copies src into dest. Dest must be zero-initialized. Source string is not consumed; caller must free it separately.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_STRING_ASSIGN
member.summary: sub_140051AB0 — glaiel::string::assign(glaiel::string* dest, const char* src, size_t len). Constructs/assigns a glaiel::string from a raw C string pointer + length. Handles both SSO (≤15 chars, inline) and heap (>15 chars) cases automatically. The dest must be zero-initialized before the first call. Pair with FN_STRING_FREE to release heap-allocated buffers.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_STRING_FREE
member.summary: sub_140052160 — glaiel::string::~string(glaiel::string* s). Releases heap-allocated string buffer when cap > 15. No-op for SSO strings. Safe to call on a zero-initialized string struct.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameAddressMap.FN_SAVE_ALL
member.summary: sub_1403BBDB0 — SaveAll(MewDirector* a1). Serializes the full game state to the SQLite save database. Internally calls sub_140203600(*MewDirector[1440], MewDirector+56) which writes the house_unlocks blob (house_type + upgrade key list) plus food/gold/collars/storage. Call with MewDirector (RVA_WORLD_PTR dereference) as the single argument. ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.Position
member.summary:
Live position on the house screen as a (X, Y) tuple.
Getter: auto-refreshes cache (non-game-thread safe); returns (NaN, NaN) when the cat is not on the house screen or the cache has not yet populated.
Setter: teleports to (X, Y). Thread-safe — auto-dispatches to the game thread. Automatically performs a cross-room container transfer when the target position is in a different room (detected by finding the live entity nearest to the target and comparing their scene-nodes). Falls back to a direct position write when no reference is available.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.IsInAdventureBox
member.summary: True when the cat is currently staged in the adventure box (pre-departure). Detected by world position: Y ≈ 1.90 and X ∈ [19, 27]. Cats in the box do not move — position is stable once staged. Returns false when position is not available (house screen not active).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.MoveToAdventureBox
member.summary: Teleports this cat into the first free slot of the adventure carton (Floor1_Small, Y=1.90). Performs a full cross-room transfer if the cat is in a different room. Thread-safe — auto-dispatches to the game thread. Returns false if all 4 slots are full or the cat is not on the house screen.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.MoveToRoomCenter(System.String)
member.summary: Teleports this cat to the center of the named room. Valid room names: "Floor1_Large", "Floor1_Small", "Attic", "Basement0" through "Basement4". Thread-safe — auto-dispatches to the game thread. Returns false if the room name is not found or the cat is not on the house screen.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.EnsurePositions(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures the position cache is fresh (non-game-thread only, at most every 200 ms). No-op when called from the game thread (would deadlock) or when cache is recent. Called automatically by the getter.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.UpdateCatPositionCacheFromPtr(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Performs the actual ECS entity-list traversal using a caller-supplied valid house ptr. Must only be called from within the FN_HOUSE_UPDATE callback context (i.e. ) where is guaranteed to be the current frame's live a1.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.HeredStat(System.Int32)
member.summary: Read one of the 7 heredity-bonus stats by zero-based index (0=STR … 6=LCK). Despite the name, these are not the genetic stats — they are the bonus points accumulated from level-ups and fights (BON1 block). They stack on top of but are not passed to offspring. Use / etc. for the breedable stats.

member.summary: Composite breeding score — how valuable this cat is as a parent. member.summary: Reads mutation slot (0–18). is a raw slot index in the character's mutation array, not a — use to look up by body part. Returns a default MutSlot (all zeros, IsEmpty=true) for empty slots.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.SetMutation(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Writes a mutation into slot (0–18) and marks it active. Use mutation IDs from . Call after modifying mutations.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.ClearMutation(System.Int32)
member.summary: Clears mutation slot (0–18) — zeroes TypeId, MutId, and Active. Call after modifying mutations.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.GetMutationByType(MewgenicsModSdk.Game.MutationSlotType)
member.summary: Returns the first non-empty mutation slot whose TypeId matches . For bilateral types (, , , ), all variant TypeIds are matched (e.g. Legs matches TypeId 11, 12, 13, or 14). Returns an empty MutSlot (IsEmpty=true) if no match is found.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.GetMutationsOfType(MewgenicsModSdk.Game.MutationSlotType)
member.summary: Returns all non-empty mutation slots whose TypeId matches . Useful for bilateral types that occupy multiple slots (e.g. ).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar.BreedWith(MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar)
member.summary:
Breed this cat with and return the resulting kitten.
Mom/dad roles are assigned automatically based on : the female (Gender=0) becomes the mom, the male (Gender≠0) becomes the dad. When both cats share the same gender this cat is treated as the mom.
The kitten is inserted into the CatDB and its visual entity is spawned so it appears in the house immediately. All genetics (stats, body parts, abilities, colour) are computed by the game engine via FN_GENETICS_COMPUTE.
Thread-safe: can be called from any thread. If not already on the game thread the call is automatically marshalled via .
Returns null when either cat is invalid, the world is not loaded, or the engine returns a null kitten pointer.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.MutationSlotType

Body-part type of a mutation slot. Use with and .
Bilateral types (Legs, Arms, Eyes, Eyebrows, Ears) may occupy multiple slots (primary + left/right secondary variants). Use to retrieve all slots of a given type.
TypeId mapping confirmed by RE of sub_14073BF00 / sub_14073BF50.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameData.ForceReloadLockedContent
member.summary:
Forces the game to re-parse data/locked_content.gon and rebuild the locked-content structures (classes, abilities, passives, items, bosses).
Typical use: call after modifying in memory so the game picks up the changes through its normal data path.
⚠ Must be called from the game thread (e.g. inside a handler). Only has effect while a save file is loaded.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameHouse
member.summary:
Typed accessor for a live glaiel::House (run-state) object in game memory.
Obtain via or , or construct directly from a house pointer captured in a hook callback.
All properties perform direct memory reads/writes — no caching. Always check before accessing any property.
Furniture API
— number of items in the inventory.
— snapshot list of all items ().
— add a new item by GON type key.
— live read/write reference () to a specific item.

member.summary: Returns all furniture items in the house inventory as live references. Properties can be read and written directly on the returned items. Returns an empty list when the house is not loaded.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameHouse.FindFurniture(System.Int64)
member.summary: Returns a live for the item with the given . Check before use — returns an invalid entry if not found.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameHouse.AddFurniture(System.String)
member.summary:
Adds a new furniture item to the house inventory by type name. The item lands in the inventory pile (posX/posY = NaN, SubName empty) — use afterwards to place it in a room.
Calls FN_ADD_FURNITURE (sub_140202560) on the game thread, which allocates a glaiel::FurniturePieceEntry, assigns a unique type_id, and appends it to the house furniture array. Returns false if the house is not loaded, the type name is empty, or the game function returns 0.

member.summary: Triggers the End Day button handler, advancing the game to the next day. Safe to call from any thread. Returns false if the house is not loaded.

example.code: // Works from any context — TCP handler, timer, etc. GameHouse.Current.AdvanceDay();

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameThread
member.summary:
Dispatches work to the game's main update thread.
The game engine is single-threaded. Any SDK method that calls native game code (memory allocation, array mutation, etc.) must run on the game thread. provides a safe bridge: callers enqueue a delegate and block until the game thread executes it.
The framework drains this queue automatically every frame — no setup required in your mod.
SDK methods like and call internally when invoked off the game thread — mod code stays simple.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameThread.IsGameThread
member.summary: True when the calling thread is the game's main update thread. SDK methods that mutate game state check this to decide whether to execute inline or marshal via .

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameThread.Drain
member.summary: Drain all pending actions. Called automatically every frame by the framework.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameThread.DrainCallback
member.summary: Unmanaged callback registered with the framework to auto-drain every frame.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameThread.Enqueue(System.Action)
member.summary: Enqueue for fire-and-forget execution on the game thread.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameThread.RunSync``1(System.Func{``0},System.Int32)
member.summary: Run on the game thread and block the calling thread until it completes (or elapses). If already on the game thread, is called inline.
member.exception: Thrown when the game thread does not drain the queue within milliseconds (default 4 000 ms).

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld
member.summary: Typed wrapper for the main glaiel::World object in game memory. Minimal subset needed by mod hooks.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.Current
member.summary: The current world state. Returns an invalid instance between runs (check ). Reads directly from game memory — always up to date.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.CurrentDay
member.summary: Absolute game-day counter — incremented once per PassDay. Starts at 0 on a new save (Jun 1). Always readable as long as the world is loaded, regardless of whether the house screen is active. Used for the cat age formula: Age = (CurrentDay − BirthDay) / 10.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.CombatType
member.summary: int32 — combat type: 0=none, 1=battle, 2=houseboss, 3=event.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.IsInCombat
member.summary: True when a combat encounter is active (any type).

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.IsInBattle
member.summary: True during a normal battle encounter.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.InCombatFlag
member.summary: Hardware combat flag set directly when combat starts (MewDirector+1812). Slightly different lifecycle from — use this for the earliest possible "combat just started" detection.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.IsProductionSave
member.summary: True when this is a real campaign/production save. False for test/dev saves. Set once during MewDirector::init and does not change during a session.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.AdventurePartyCount
member.summary: Number of cats currently in the adventure party (populated from save data when an adventure is active; 0 when no adventure has departed or save was not yet loaded).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.IsInAdventureParty(System.Int64)
member.summary: Returns true if is present in the engine's adventure-party array (MewDirector+1468/1472). This is reliable after a save-load with an active adventure; for newly staged adventures in the same session the party array may not yet be populated.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.ChapterName
member.summary: Current map or chapter identifier string (e.g. "chapter_map", "AREA_NAME_HOUSE"). Read-only — changing this does not reload the map. Returns empty string when no world is loaded.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.ChapterNum
member.summary: Current chapter number / difficulty level. Read-only — setting this mid-game has no effect on the loaded map.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.ChapterState
member.summary: Chapter progression state. 0 = initial/unvisited, 1 = entered. Can be written to nudge progression tracking.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.GamePhase
member.summary: Global game phase enum. Values 5 and 8 observed in RE. Exact meaning TBD. Read-only — do not write without understanding the full phase state machine.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.EventDifficulty
member.summary: World event difficulty counter. Cleared to 0 before each event, incremented during event setup, then copied into the battle struct (+6772). Writable — set before combat begins to override event difficulty.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.LockedContent
member.summary: Locked content sub-struct (classes, abilities, items, bosses).

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.CatDbPtr
member.summary: Raw pointer to the CatDatabase object. Used as first argument to FN_CATDB_FIND / FN_DELETE_CAT. Returns 0 if no game is loaded.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.ForEachCat(System.Action{System.Int64,System.IntPtr})
member.summary:
Iterates every node in the CatDB linked list and invokes with (catId, charPtr) for each live entry.
The callback is invoked on the caller's thread — use inside game event handlers only.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.GetCats
member.summary: Returns all live (non-dead) cats as a list of wrappers. Dead/removed cats are skipped automatically. Use to iterate the full CatDB without any filter.

example.code: foreach (var cat in GameWorld.Current.GetCats()) Log($"{cat.Name} Lv.{cat.Level} {cat.ClassName}");

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.GetPlayerCats
member.summary: Returns all live player cats (IsPlayer == true, not dead).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.GetCatById(System.Int64)
member.summary: Finds a cat by its unique ID and returns a wrapper. Returns null if the ID is not in the CatDB.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.GetAllCats
member.summary: Returns every entry in the CatDB as a — including dead/removed cats. Use for the common case of iterating only live cats.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.DeleteCat(System.Int64)
member.summary:
Permanently removes a cat from the game by cat ID.
⚠️ MUST be called from GameEvents.OnHouseUpdate (fires post-trampoline — safe window). Calling from other contexts risks renderer crashes.
The method handles both at-home cats (immediate delete via ECS entity cleanup) and cats without a renderer entity (direct CatDB removal). Automatically removes the catId from world petList / catList2 to prevent use-after-free in the next frame.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.DeleteCat(MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameChar)
member.summary: Deletes a cat by its wrapper. Called by .

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.AddCatToAdventureBox(System.Int64)
member.summary: Moves a cat into the physical adventure carton by teleporting it to the first unoccupied slot position (21.50–24.50, Y=1.90). Uses FN_FIND_CONTAINER_BY_POS for the cross-room transfer — works even when Floor1_Small is currently empty. Returns false if all 4 slots are full or the entity cannot be found.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.MoveToRoomCenter(System.Int64,System.String)
member.summary: Uses FN_FIND_CONTAINER_BY_NAME to locate the container, then reads the room origin from its scene_node (container+56 → scene_node, +128/+136 = X/Y). Room names: "Floor1_Large", "Floor1_Small", "Attic", "Basement0"–"Basement4". ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.SmartTeleportCat(System.Int64,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Smart teleport: moves to (targetX, targetY) on the house screen. Uses FN_FIND_CONTAINER_BY_POS (the game's own drag-drop resolver) to identify which room container owns the target coordinates — no reference cat in that room required. Performs a full cross-room container transfer if the target room differs from the source room. ⚠ Must run on the game thread.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.SceneNodeForContainer(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the scene_node pointer for the given container. Reads from the container's first entity if it has any; falls back to the warm cache.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GameWorld.GetCatPhysDiag(System.Int64)
member.summary: Dumps NpcMapEntity + HouseCatPhysics raw fields for a cat (RE diagnostic). Returns null if the cat or physics ptr is not found. ⚠ Call from the game thread.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GlaielWstring
member.summary:
Helpers for reading/writing glaiel engine string types from native memory.
glaiel::wstring (UTF-16, 32-byte struct): [+0 ..+15] inline wchar_t buffer OR *(ptr) = heap wchar_t* when capacity > 7 [+16..+23] uint64 size (wide char count, NOT bytes) [+24..+31] uint64 capacity (≤7 = SSO/inline, >7 = heap)
glaiel::string (ASCII, 32-byte struct): [+0 ..+15] inline char buffer OR *(ptr) = heap char* when capacity > 15 [+16..+23] uint64 size [+24..+31] uint64 capacity (≤15 = SSO/inline, >15 = heap)

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GlaielWstring.ReadWide(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Read UTF-16 glaiel::wstring at .

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GlaielWstring.ReadAscii(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Read ASCII glaiel::string at .

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GlaielWstring.WriteAscii(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Overwrites the ASCII glaiel::string at with . Handles SSO (≤ 15 bytes inline) and existing heap allocations. Allocates a new heap buffer via Marshal.AllocHGlobal when capacity must grow. //TEST

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GlaielWstring.WriteWide(System.IntPtr,System.String)
M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GlaielWstring.AppendWide(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary:
Appends to the glaiel::wstring at .
Safety constraints: - skips if (newSize+1)*2 >= 0x1000 (game destructor uses ptr[-8] for large allocs) - allocates via Marshal.AllocHGlobal (= HeapAlloc(GetProcessHeap())) — compatible with game's malloc which also uses the default heap - frees old heap buffer if it was heap-allocated

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Gon
M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Gon.From(System.String)
member.summary:
Reads a GON file from live native GPAK memory and returns a mutable node tree. Every call deserialises the current state of game memory — reads always reflect any writes made via .
Also returns nodes registered via — those share the same managed instance so modifications are preserved.
Returns an empty root node with the path context set — calling on the result will register it for future calls.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Gon.ReapplyIntoSpawnDbOnce(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Gon.SpawnDbInitRva
member.summary: RVA of the SpawnDB initializer (sub_1407A4910). Hook this with MewgenicsMod.Hook() and call after each invocation so injected content survives Level::init catalog rebuilds.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Gon.AttachPassiveRva
member.summary: RVA of the REAL glaiel::attach_passive dispatch (sub_1404905B0, ConstructionHelpers.cpp). Signature: glaiel::Passive* __fastcall(glaiel_string* name, glaiel::Character* charPtr, const GonObject* gon, bool flag) Maps built-in effect-type names ("StrengthUp", "Thorns", etc.) to C++ Passive objects. Logs "NO PASSIVE NAMED X" when X is not a known built-in type. Hook this to intercept custom named passive definitions stored in SpawnDB passives catalog.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.Gon.SpawnDbGetRva
member.summary: RVA of sub_1403194B0 — the per-character SpawnDB get-or-create function. Called once per character by combat init (sub_14082FB70). Returns the SpawnDB* that attach_passive will read from. Has a fast-path that returns a cached SpawnDB without calling FN_SPAWN_DB_INIT (so that hook may never fire on subsequent combats). Hooking the return of THIS function is the only reliable injection point. Signature: SpawnDatabase* __fastcall(TacticsTest* a1, nint a2, nint a3)

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GpakReader
member.summary:
Provides access to GPAK file content — memory-only, loaded once at startup.
Walks the game's in-process GPAK hash map at init time and stores: • GON files → native GonObject pointer (stable for the lifetime of the process) • CSV files → pre-serialised text (read once; immutable at runtime)
uses the cached pointer to deserialise the GON tree directly from live native memory on every call — no stale C# snapshot. Writes go directly to native game memory via — this cache is never involved in write operations.
Usage: use from mod code — GpakReader is an internal detail.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GpakReader.FileCount
member.summary: Total number of data/ entries loaded from the in-process GPAK.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GpakReader.GonFileCount
member.summary: Number of GON entries loaded (CSV-only state = GPAK not fully ready).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GpakReader.LoadFromGameDir
member.summary:
Walks the game's in-process GPAK hash map and caches all "data/" entries. Cached after the first successful (non-empty) load.
Returns null if SharedGameBase is not yet set or if the GPAK is not yet initialised (which happens at DLL-load time, before the game's startup code runs). In that case no result is cached so subsequent calls will retry automatically.

member.summary: Returns the cached native GonObject pointer for a GON file. Returns false if the path is not a known GON file.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonReader
member.summary:
Reads GON/text content directly from the game's in-process parsed GonObject trees, by calling FN_GPAK_READ_GON and serializing the returned tree back to text.
GonObject C++ layout (MSVC x64, verified against disassembly of Mewgenics.exe):
+0x00 std::unordered_map<string,int> children_map — 56 bytes (skipped; use children_array) +0x38 std::vector<GonObject> children_array — 24 bytes: [_begin*, _end*, _cap*] +0x50 int int_data +0x54 (4 bytes padding) +0x58 double float_data +0x60 bool bool_data +0x61 (7 bytes padding) +0x68 std::string string_data — 32 bytes MSVC SSO +0x88 std::string name — 32 bytes MSVC SSO +0xA8 FieldType type — int32 enum sizeof = 0xB0 = 176 bytes
std::string / glaiel::string SSO layout (32 bytes): +0x00 inline_buf[16] OR heap ptr (8 bytes ptr + 8 bytes buf continuation) +0x10 size (uint64) +0x18 capacity (uint64); if <= 0xF → SSO inline, else → heap
FN_GPAK_READ_GON return value: ptr → some GpakEntry wrapper; root GonObject starts at ptr + 0x28.
FieldType enum: NULLGON=0, STRING=1, NUMBER=2, OBJECT=3, ARRAY=4, BOOL=5
Verification: "mov eax,[rbx+0xA8]" (FieldType check in caller) → type at +0xA8 ✓ "mov r15,[r12+0x38]" (children_array._begin) → children at +0x38 ✓

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonReader.WalkAll(System.IntPtr,System.Action{System.String,System.IntPtr})
member.summary:
Walks the GPAK global hash-map linked list and calls for every "gon_data/…" and "csv_data/…" entry.
For GON entries the callback receives (path, gonObjectPtr) where gonObjectPtr = resource + 0x28. For CSV entries the callback receives (path, csvResourcePtr) where csvResourcePtr = resource base.
The caller can distinguish them by path extension (.gon vs .csv).
Returns false if the GPAK is not yet initialised (count == 0 or sentinel == null). In that case the caller should NOT cache the (empty) result and should retry later.
Safety: • No native functions are called — purely reads already-mapped memory. • Guarded by entry-count and iteration caps so a corrupt list can't loop forever. • Key sizes capped at 4 KB; resource ptr must be non-null.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonReader.ReadCsvAsText(System.IntPtr)
member.summary:
Reads the localization data stored inside a CSV GonResource.
CsvResource layout (vtable 0x7FF765A1BFC8, different from GON vtable): +0x00 vtable +0x08 glaiel_string path (32 bytes) → ends at +0x28 +0x28 vector.begin (ptr to first 24-byte element) +0x30 vector.end (ptr past last element) +0x38 vector.cap
Each vector element (24 bytes = 3 QWORDs): QWORD[0] = rowPtr → pointer to row struct: rowPtr + 0 : glaiel_string key (32 bytes) rowPtr + 32 : glaiel_string value_en (32 bytes, English / first language column) rowPtr + 64+: more language columns (ignored) QWORD[1] = (unknown — possibly hash or index, ignored) QWORD[2] = (unknown, ignored)
Verified via CheatEngine: element 0 key="KEY" (header), element 1 key="CURRENT_LANGUAGE_NAME" value_en="English".

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonReader.SerializeGonNode(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Serializes a single named GonObject node and all its children recursively using linear children scan (not hash lookup). Used for diagnostic dumps.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonReader.ReadGlaielString(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Reads a glaiel_string at the given pointer. Public for diagnostic use.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonReader.SerializeRoot(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Serializes the GonObject at to GON text. Called by GpakReader after walking the GPAK hash map.

member.summary: Appends "name value" or "name {\n...\n}" at the given indent depth.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter
member.summary:
Writes string values directly into the game's native C++ GonObject tree. Complements NativeGonReader for in-memory GPAK modification.
GonObject layout (sizeof=0xB0, verified via disassembly): +0x38 children vector.begin +0x40 children vector.end +0x68 string_data (glaiel_string, 32 bytes) +0x88 name (glaiel_string, 32 bytes) +0xA8 FieldType (int32: 0=NULL, 1=STRING, 2=NUMBER, 3=OBJECT, 4=ARRAY, 5=BOOL)
glaiel_string (32 bytes, MSVC SSO): +0x00 inline_buf[16] OR heap ptr (8 bytes) +0x10 size (uint64) +0x18 capacity (uint64); if <= 0xF → SSO inline, else → heap ptr at +0x00

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.SetString(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Finds the GonObject for in the live GPAK and sets the string value of the node at (e.g. "ScrapHat/desc").
Returns true on success, false if the path was not found.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.SetNumber(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Finds the GonObject for in the live GPAK and sets the integer value of the node at (e.g. "ScrapHat/str").
Writes both int_data (+0x50) and float_data (+0x58), and sets type to NUMBER. Returns true on success, false if the path was not found.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.SetNumberInSpawnDb(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Scans all 28 embedded GonObject slots in SpawnDatabase and patches every slot that contains a node matching .
This is the correct way to propagate a GPAK number patch to SpawnDB without re-calling the full catalog loader (which crashes due to secondary structure accumulation — "Class X defined in multiple places").
Works for any catalog type (items, characters, abilities, classes, etc.) without needing a file-path → SD-offset mapping.
Returns true if at least one slot was patched.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.SetStringInSpawnDb(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Scans all 28 embedded GonObject slots in SpawnDatabase and patches every slot that contains a node matching with a string value.
Returns true if at least one slot was patched.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.GetNumber(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reads the current integer value of a NUMBER node in the live native GPAK. Returns if the node is not found or is not a NUMBER.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.InjectGon(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.String)
M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.InjectGonIfMissing(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary:
Like but skips top-level blocks that already exist in the catalog (checked via the game's own hash lookup). Use for idempotent re-injection after a SpawnDB rebuild wipes in-memory injections.
Returns the number of new entries actually injected (0 if all already present).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.InjectGon(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GonNode)
member.summary: Injects a single pre-parsed into . Returns 1 on success, 0 on failure.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.InjectGonIfMissing(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GonNode)
member.summary: Like but skips if the entry already exists. Returns 1 if injected, 0 if already present or on failure.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.RebuildHashTablesRecursive(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Recursively rebuilds the hash table of and all its descendant OBJECT nodes. This is required after sub_1400285E0 (the deep-copy inside the game's insert_child) skips the hash-table fields, leaving every copied node with an empty Swiss table. Without this, any nested field lookup (e.g. cost["act_points"]) returns the "not found" sentinel and the game calls sub_14093CC90 which aborts with a GON error.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.NativeBuildGonObject(System.IntPtr,MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GonNode)
member.summary: Allocates and initialises a native GonObject from . For OBJECT nodes every child is recursively inserted via insert_child. For leaf nodes (STRING / NUMBER) only the scalar fields are written. The caller is responsible for eventually passing this to a native function that deep-copies it (e.g. insert_child), after which this allocation can be leaked.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.NativeInsertChild(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.String,System.IntPtr)
member.summary:
Calls the game's native insert_child(parent, key, child) (RVA 0x941170).
The function deep-copies into 's managed children array, inserts into the hash table with correct Abseil SIMD ctrl byte cloning, and handles all vector growth internally.
Key ownership: the native function destructs the key string (calls its dtor) before returning. We therefore pass an SSO string for keys ≤ 15 bytes (safe — dtor is a no-op) or a game-malloc'd buffer for longer keys.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.NativeLookupEntry(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary:
Calls the game's native hash lookup (sub_14093ED00, RVA 0x93ED00) on to locate . Returns (found=true, type=FieldType, ptr=child GonObject*) if the entry exists in the catalog's Swiss table, or (found=false, type=0, ptr=sentinel) if not found.
This uses the SAME lookup function the game calls at combat time — a false result here definitively means the hash table was not updated after injection.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.GetCatalogStats(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the number of entries in the catalog's Swiss-table hash (at GonObject+0x10) and the number of children in the children vector (= (children_end − children_begin) / GonObject_size).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.BuildGonObject(System.IntPtr,MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GonNode)
member.summary: Recursively builds a native GonObject at from a . All allocations use VirtualAlloc so they never conflict with the game's CRT heap.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.CloneItem(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Shallow-clones 's GonObject (copies the 0xB0-byte struct verbatim) into a new slot, writes as the clone's name, then appends it to the catalog and inserts a hash entry.
Because the clone shares its metadata/entries/children pointers with the source, the new item shows the exact same name, stats, and effects as the source — only its lookup key differs. Use SetString / SetNumber on the clone afterward to diverge values.
Returns true on success; false if sourceId is not found or memory allocation fails.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.FindGonObjPtr(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Walks the GPAK to find the GonObject root ptr for a file path.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.NavigateTo(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Navigates a slash-separated subpath within a GonObject tree.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.FindChild(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Finds the first child GonObject whose name matches (case-insensitive).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.WriteGlaielString(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Writes a UTF-8 string into a glaiel_string at . Handles three cases: 1. New value fits in SSO (≤ 15 bytes): write inline 2. New value fits in existing heap capacity: write in-place 3. Requires more space: allocate a new VirtualAlloc buffer

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.InsertHashEntry(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Inserts a new key → childIndex mapping into the GonObject Swiss-table hash map.
Algorithm mirrors the game's own hash_lookup (sub_14093ED00): h = FNV-1a(key) h2 = high-64 of (h × 0xDE5FB9D2630458E9) ← finalization fingerprint = h2 & 0x7F initial_slot = (h2 >> 7) & mask Probe forward until an empty slot (control byte == 0x80) is found. Write control byte = fingerprint, entry = glaiel_string(key) + int(childIndex).
Precondition: the table must have at least one free slot (control byte 0x80). The game always leaves sufficient slack (items loaded at ~55 % load factor).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeGonWriter.ComputeSwissHash(System.String)
member.summary:
Computes the finalized 64-bit hash used by the game's Swiss-table lookup. Matches the computation in hash_lookup (sub_14093ED00): FNV-1a over UTF-8 bytes → (__PAIR128__(h, h) * 0xDE5FB9D2630458E9) >> 64
__PAIR128__(h, h) = h * (2^64 + 1), so the full expression is: (high64(h * k) + low64(h * k)) mod 2^64 where k = 0xDE5FB9D2630458E9.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeTextTable
member.summary:
Runtime text/localization injection — pure in-memory, no file required.
Architecture (confirmed by deep RE of sub_14095DCD0 and sub_140986B70): Language map ( = dword_1413B8C90):
outer hash map (keyed by language code "en", "fr", etc.) → outer_node (+0x30 = inner hash map) → inner_node (+0x30 = glaiel_wstring UTF-16 display text)
Injection (3 native calls per language): 1. (langMap, scratch, langCode) → outer_node 2. (outer_node+0x30, scratch, textKey) → inner_node 3. (inner_node+0x30, utf16_ptr, len)
Keys are injected into the active language and "en" (debug/fallback). Current language is read from .

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeTextTable.InjectText(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Injects all into the game's live language map immediately. Pure in-memory — no files on disk.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.NativeTextTable.InjectText(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Convenience overload for a single key/value pair.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase
member.summary:
Wraps the native SpawnDatabase singleton — the game's pre-parsed item catalog.
SpawnDatabase's items catalog GonObject lives at: sd_ptr + GameAddressMap.SPAWN_DB_ITEMS_OFFSET (0x5B8) Its children are all items merged from data/items/ (armor_sets, weapons, accessories, etc.).
To update an item stat after a NativeGonWriter GPAK patch, write directly to SpawnDB's GonObject via NativeGonWriter.SetNumberAt / SetStringAt. Do NOT call the full catalog loader (FN_SPAWN_DB_INIT) at runtime — secondary structures (class registry, etc.) accumulate instead of being replaced, causing "Class X defined in multiple places" crash.
The global singleton pointer lives at: gameBase + GameAddressMap.RVA_SPAWN_DB_INSTANCE → SpawnDatabase*

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.GetInstancePtr(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Returns the native SpawnDatabase singleton pointer, or 0 if not yet initialised.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.PersistentInjections
member.summary: All (catalog, node) pairs registered via . Re-applied after each SpawnDB rebuild (Level::init wipes in-memory injections).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.ReapplyAllInjections(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Re-injects all previously registered GON entries into the global SpawnDB, skipping entries that are already present (idempotent).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.ReapplyIntoSpawnDb(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary:
Re-injects all previously registered GON entries into (any SpawnDB — global or per-combat), skipping already-present entries.
Use this in the FN_SPAWN_DB_INIT hook to inject into the per-combat SpawnDB that Level::init creates fresh from GPAK each time.

member.name: F:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase._injectedSpawnDbs
member.summary: Set of SpawnDB pointers that have already received all persistent injections. Used by to skip repeated injections for the same SpawnDB instance (avoids per-passive hash lookups after the first call). Cleared when PersistentInjections grows (new content registered by a mod).

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.ReapplyIntoSpawnDbOnce(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.summary:
Like but tracks which SpawnDB pointers have already been fully re-injected. Subsequent calls for the same are no-ops until new injections are registered (PersistentInjections grows).
Use this in the FN_ATTACH_PASSIVE hook — called once per passive per character during Level::init, so avoiding redundant hash lookups is worthwhile.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.RegisterGon(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Parses and injects every top-level block into the named catalog's live GonObject in SpawnDB.
must be one of: items, abilities, ability_templates, passives, classes, characters, events, shops, mutations, maps
Returns the number of top-level items injected, or -1 if the catalog name is unknown.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.RegisterGon(System.IntPtr,System.String,MewgenicsModSdk.Game.GonNode)
member.summary: Registers a single pre-parsed into the named catalog and persists it for re-injection after SpawnDB rebuilds. Returns 1 if injected, -1 if catalog unknown, 0 if SpawnDB not ready.

member.name: M:MewgenicsModSdk.Game.SpawnDatabase.GetCatalogGon(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the GonObject* for the named catalog, or 0 if unknown.

member.name: T:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod
member.summary:
Base class for Mewgenics mods. Derive from this in your mod project.
Your NativeAOT mod DLL must also provide the C-ABI exports — use to generate them automatically with minimal boilerplate.
Lifecycle: 1. — called once when the mod DLL is loaded by the core 2. — called when the mod is activated 3. — called when the mod is deactivated

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.Id
member.summary: Unique identifier for this mod (e.g. "infinite_gold").

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.Name
member.summary: Display name shown in mod managers.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.Version
member.summary: Semantic version string (e.g. "1.0.0").

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.Description
member.summary: Short description of what this mod does.

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.Category
member.summary: Category shown in the overlay (e.g. "Gameplay", "Quality of Life").

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.AutoEnable
member.summary: If true, the mod will be automatically enabled when loaded. Override and return true for mods that should always be active. Default: false (user must enable via overlay).

member.name: P:MewgenicsModSdk.MewgenicsMod.IsEnabled
member.summary: Whether the mod is currently enabled.
